channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Chinese navy helicopter flies within 10 feet of Philippine patrol plane over disputed shoal
item.description: A Chinese navy helicopter flew within 10 feet of a Philippine patrol plane on Tuesday in a disputed area of the the South China Sea, as the Filipino pilot warned by radio: “You are flying too close, you are very dangerous.”.
item.dc:creator: Joeal Calupitan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Top Russian and US officials meet for talks on Ukraine war without Kyiv
item.description: Top Russian and American officials met in Saudi Arabia on Tuesday to begin talks on improving ties and negotiating an end to the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Baraa Anwer And Dasha Litvinova, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Philippine Vice President Duterte asks Supreme Court to void her impeachment and block Senate trial
item.description: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte has asked the Supreme Court to void her impeachment and block a Senate trial that could remove her from office.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korea slams US and Asian rivals for pursuing 'absurd' plans to denuclearize North
item.description: North Korea has criticized the United States and Asian neighbors for pursuing the “absurd” goal of denuclearizing the North and says it will push to expand its nuclear forces under the direction of its authoritarian leader Kim Jong Un.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels tighten their grip on second major city in eastern Congo
item.description: BUKAVU, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels tightened their grip on Bukavu on Monday, a day after seizing the second major city in eastern Congo whose residents appeared resigned to their fate under the new rulers.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL CINAMULA and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Top Russian and American officials will hold talks on ending the Ukraine war without Kyiv
item.description: RIYADH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — Senior American and Russian officials, including the countries’ top diplomats, will hold talks on improving their ties and negotiating an end to the war in Ukraine, officials said Monday, in what would be the most significant meeting between the sides since Moscow's full-
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, JUSTIN SPIKE and BARRY HATTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz says he's 'in touch with Delta' after crash landing at Toronto airport
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) said Monday he was “in touch with Delta” following the crash landing of a flight at Toronto Pearson International Airport. The flight originated at Minneapolis-St. Paul International Airport (MSP) in Walz's state.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israelis and Palestinians mark 500 days of war in Gaza with more uncertainty
item.description: Israelis have marked 500 days of war in Gaza with protests demanding further progress on a ceasefire so all remaining hostages can be returned. People demonstrated in Tel Aviv and Jerusalem on Monday as less than two weeks remain in the ceasefire's current phase.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump speaks with Macron ahead of European leaders meeting on Ukraine
item.description: President Trump spoke with French President Emmanuel Macron ahead of a European leaders' meeting about the war in Ukraine, as the Trump administration is pressing forward with plans to negotiate an end to the war.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Argentine judge investigates fraud case against President Milei over crypto promotion
item.description: A judge in Argentina has been selected to investigate allegations of fraud against President Javier Milei for his brief promotion of a cryptocurrency that collapsed in value hours after it was launched last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Border town's residents rebuild in south Lebanon as Hezbollah leader calls for Israeli withdrawal
item.description: Residents of a south Lebanon town are waiting to rebuild and fix their damaged homes as Israel is supposed to end its military presence north of its border under a U.S.-brokered deal.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Taiwan cheers removal of ‘we do not support Taiwan independence’ from State Department website
item.description: Taiwan expressed approval after the U.S. State Department removed a statement on its website that said it does not support the island's independence. The phrase “we do not support Taiwan independence” was removed from a department fact sheet last week. The document, which highlights U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Egypt is developing a plan to rebuild Gaza, countering Trump's call to depopulate the territory
item.description: Egypt is developing a plan to rebuild Gaza without forcing Palestinians out of the strip in a counter to President Donald Trump’s proposal to depopulate the territory so the U.S. can take it over.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Watch live: Senators discuss trip to Israel from Tel Aviv
item.description: A bipartisan group of senators led by Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) will hold a press conference Monday from Tel Aviv to discuss their trip to Israel. The remarks are scheduled to begin at 12:30 p.m. EST and follow a number of meetings the group had with Israeli government officials.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Retired general: Putin will likely 'wait out' Trump term and take Ukraine
item.description: Retired U.S. Army Gen. Jack Keane said he suspects Russian President Vladimir Putin will wait for President Trump to leave office before trying to topple the government in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Here are some takeaways from the first month of Trump's Mideast diplomacy
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — When Hamas threatened to call off the planned release of three Israeli hostages last week, U.S. President Donald Trump stepped into the picture with an unexpected ultimatum.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli military experts weigh in on Trump's ‘all hell’ threat to Hamas and what it could look like
item.description: Former Israeli military officials discuss how President Donald Trump's threats against Hamas could play out in Gaza, and what strategies the IDF could adopt if they go back into the enclave.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China says US has 'gravely backpedaled' on Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's Foreign Ministry took issue Monday with a revised U.S. government fact sheet that removed a line on American opposition to independence for Taiwan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu signals he's moving ahead with Trump's idea to transfer Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday signaled that he was moving ahead with U.S. President Donald Trump's proposal to transfer the Palestinian population out of Gaza, calling it “the only viable plan to enable a different future" for the region.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Food delivery driver helped stop suspect following deadly Austria stabbing: officials
item.description: A 23-year-old with possible connections to the Islamic State group is in custody after a stabbing of six passersby that left a 14-year-old boy dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Ukrainian spiritual leader says Russian Orthodox Church extension of Kremlin
item.description: A senior Ukrainian Orthodox Church official recently warned that Russian President Vladimir Putin thinks the Kremlin is in a 'holy war' with the West.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White South Africans gather in support of Trump and his claims that they are victims of racism
item.description: PRETORIA, South Africa (AP) — Some white South Africans showed support for President Donald Trump on Saturday and gathered at the U.S. Embassy in Pretoria to claim they are victims of racism by their own government.
item.dc:creator: ALFONSO NQUNJANA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Students in Iran continue protests over 19-year-old’s murder on campus for second day
item.description: Student protesters in Iran called out officials at the University of Tehran for a second day on Saturday following the murder of a business student on campus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu will reportedly hold a security meeting at Trump's deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu is expected to hold a security meeting at 12 p.m. eastern, President Trump's deadline for Hamas to release the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Crunch time for hostages amid ceasefire uncertainty
item.description: Fox News Correspondent Greg Palkot spoke with the daughter and sister of two released hostages. Both noted the positive role played by President Trump in helping to free their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kentucky bourbon bottles signed by the pope raise thousands for charity
item.description: It sounds like the opening line of a joke: What happens when the pope gets his hands on two bottles of Kentucky bourbon?
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Taiwan pledges to communicate and invest more in the US after Trump tariff threats
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan President Lai Ching-te said Friday he would communicate more with the U.S. over President Donald Trump’s concerns over the chip industry and invest more in the U.S, hours after Trump ordered reciprocal tariffs on trade partners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Gas explosion at Taiwan food court kills 4 and injures 26
item.description: A gas explosion at a department store in Taiwan on Thursday killed four people and injured 26, fire authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Steel tariffs could disrupt international bridge expansion
item.description: The steel 2-lane Starr-Camargo International Bridge in Rio Grande City, Texas, is gearing up for an expansion of lanes, however they might not be steel after 25% tariffs were imposed by the Trump administration, the bridge's owner Sam Vale told Border Report on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Nearly everyone in the world breathes bad air. How to lower your risk
item.description: Polluted air, laden noxious gasses or tiny, invisible particles that burrow into human bodies, kills 7 million people prematurely every year, the U.N. health agency estimates.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: King Abdullah says Jordan will bring in 2K 'very ill' Palestinian children
item.description: Trump is not backing down from his plans for the future of Gaza and is pressuring Jordan, Egypt and other Arab countries to take in Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he may withhold aid to Jordan, Egypt if they don’t take in Palestinians
item.description: President Trump said Monday he would consider withholding aid to Jordan and Egypt if they do not agree to take in Palestinians as part of a plan for the U.S. to take over and develop the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas' threat to delay hostage release raises fears for ceasefire
item.description: The next handover of three hostages had been scheduled for Saturday, and families say time is running out for those still alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
